item.title: STAFF POST - OPEN Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Show Reasons
– hide block

The cross-links below are being proposed due to the nature of 2347, and the fact that the article does not clarify whether or not 2347 is capable of reassembling from non self immolation events.

Editing this:

Once SCP-2347 has completed the combustion event, it will enter Phase 2. All bony structures remaining will maintain a maximum distance of 0.8 meters from each other and will slowly move in order to reconstruct a normal human skeletal configuration over the course of 24 hours.

To this:

Once SCP-2347 has completed the combustion event, it will enter Phase 2. All bony structures remaining will maintain a maximum distance of 0.8 meters from each other and will slowly move in order to reconstruct a normal human skeletal configuration over the course of 24 hours.1

Footnotes
1. It is currently not known whether SCP-2347 would regenerate in this manner following immolation from other fire based anomalies, such as SCP-1179 or SCP-1641.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good SCP. Unfortunately the phrase "exothermic self-resurrection" makes mme think of Doctor Who, which was a bit offputting.

Minor nitpick:

All soft tissues will increase in temperature to a minimum of 150ºC in less than 1 seconds and will maintain this temperature until all soft tissues have been converted to ash.

I think it should be changed to "less than 1 second" (remove the plural).

Neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty sure there's already a human phoenix (female, as I recall). Furthermore, nothing about this is terribly interesting. It's just a guy who SHC's and then comes back to life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…but are unable to prevent the consumption of SCP-2347.

Is this meant to be "combustion"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My view is colored somewhat by the fact that we already have a ton of "wow, sucks to be that guy" articles around here. It's not bad, but there isn't a whole lot here beyond how much it indeed sucks to be this guy.

I do like the uncluttered nature of the narrative and the absence of a shoehorned back story or goofy interview log, so without any larger context I would probably be a neutral vote on this. Ultimately, though, I think this is ground we've covered before, and there needs to be something new if we're to keep doing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to know that Maxwell's Phoenix is back up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …Honestly, I have to hand it to you, you raise a pretty good point. Not knowing what this guy looks like does add an extra element of horror to this skip. Well argued.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. It really sucks to be this guy. Like, really, really sucks. Take your +1, you magnificent beary monster.

To counterpoint what Dr Leonard said above, I think not having the additional characteristics only feeds more fridge horror to this article. This poor guy could be anybody. Young, old, European, African…whatever. It works like Spiderman's mask. If you don't know Spiderman is Peter Parker, then all you know is he's a male. He could be anyone. Your brother, cousin, father, the nice baker down the street or that jerk at the gas station that always short-changes you. Not including those details, I think, really adds to the awfulness of this poor sap's existence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really do have a thing for thermodynamics SCPs, and this manages to do both that and a horror element without any unnecessary elements. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's part of what I was trying to imply. The other is that this guy's environment needs to be around 130 degrees Farenheit for him to not rapidly immolate, but he feels every degree of that heat, and he still eventually burns up. Which he feels. At least once a month on average.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the fact that he experiences pain when he combusts, as MrWrong stated, the selling point of this is the depiction of a cold Foundation. I fixed a grammatical error for you, although I might just note that, although it's certainly not required when writing a humanoid SCP, it's usually nice to know a bit more about its physical characteristics - its age/ethnicity, for example. Like I said, it's not necessary, but it's nice to have.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe we have seen this before. In any case, I now realise a fridge horror moment – for the Foundation to have such details over its anomalous process, it must have observed the process many times over to pen it down.

I would say the selling point is this implication of a cold Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nothing like freezing to death while you're suffering heat stroke, or burning to death while you finally feel comfortable.

EDIT: Also, this is a partial rewrite/updating of a SCP I posted a while ago. Hopefully I addressed the concerns raised at that time.
